content.claim: Church-maintained and private schools operate under exemptions from state admissions rules and may set their own operational criteria, and in 2015 the Hungarian Curia exempted Greek Catholic Church from anti-discrimination provisions, permitting segregated Roma-only schools
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Church/private-school selection operates independently of 6/8-year gimnazium policy and would persist under any tracking reform
Curia decision is normative/legal (what is permitted), not descriptive of actual prevalence of segregated church schools
Ercse characterization of 'state-motivated church-assisted segregation' is an advocacy interpretation, contested in framing
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
